gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (1/353)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (1/353)
text: سُئِلَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) عَنِ الِاسْتِدْرَاجِ فَقَالَ هُوَ الْعَبْدُ يُذْنِبُ الذَّنْبَ فَيُمْلَى لَهُ وَ تُجَدَّدُ لَهُ عِنْدَهَا النِّعَمُ فَتُلْهِيهِ عَنِ الِاسْتِغْفَارِ مِنَ الذُّنُوبِ فَهُوَ مُسْتَدْرَجٌ مِنْ حَيْثُ لَا يَعْلَمُ .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Sahl Bin Ziya and Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, altogether from Ibn Mahboub, from Ibn Ra’ib, from one of his companions who said,

‘Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> was asked about the gradual allurement, so he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘He is the servant who commits the sin, so he is Respited for it and the Bounty is renewed for him during it, and it distracts him from the seeking of Forgiveness from the sins. So this is the gradual allurement from where he does not know’.
en.sarwar: 2. A number of our people have narrated from Sahl ibn Ziyad and Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father all from ibn Mahbub from ibn Ri’ab from certain individuals of his people who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) was asked about al-‘Istidraj, (gradual steps). The Imam said, ‘It happens when a servant (of Allah) commits a sin he is given respite, new bounties come to him and he overlooks beseeching forgiveness for sins, thus, he is taken in gradual steps but he has not taken any notice of it.’”
